# Chinese salad dressing

<http://www.food.com/recipe/asian-vinaigrette-salad-dressing-114822>

---

# Asian Vinaigrette Salad Dressing

[SVG ImageSVG ImageSVG ImageSVG ImageSVG ImageSVG ImageSVG ImageSVG ImageSVG ImageSVG Image 33](#reviews)

[Community Pick](/recipe/all/popular)

[![](https://img.sndimg.com/food/image/upload/v1/gk-static/gk/img/avatar/sushi.png)](https://www.food.com/user/168158)

Submitted by [DangerBun](https://www.food.com/user/168158)

"This is simple to throw together, light and tangy. I often vary the ingredients, using honey or orange juice instead of sugar, adding wasabi powder -- whatever I'm in the mood for.

The joy of cooking is being creative!

If you wish to substitute the oil, use a bland vegetable oil, extra virgin olive oil or "light" olive oil. Regular olive oil is too strong for this recipe."

9

![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)Serves:
6-8

![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Nutrition information

## Ingredients

Units: US

- 3 tablespoons rice wine vinegar
- 1 tablespoon [sugar](/about/sugar-139)
- 3 tablespoons [soy sauce](/about/soy-sauce-473), pref. low-sodium
- 1 tablespoon [ginger](/about/ginger-166), fresh grated
- 1⁄2 teaspoon [garlic](/about/garlic-165), fresh minced
- 2 tablespoons sesame oil
- 1⁄2 cup peanut oil or 1/2 cup grapeseed oil
- 1 tablespoon [sesame seeds](/about/sesame-seed-344), lightly toasted
- 1 tablespoon [scallion](/about/green-onion-363), chopped (green onions)

Advertisement

## Instructions

- Mix first 5 ingredients in a bowl or food processor.
- If using a bowl: SLOWLY drizzle in the sesame and peanut oil, whisking constantly so that the dressing will emulsify.
- If using a food processor, leave it running while you drizzle in the oil.
- When dressing is well combined, add sesame seeds and scallions.
- Serve immediately or refrigerate and use within a week.

[![icons / social / pinterest](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)](<//www.pinterest.com/pin/create/button/?media=https://img.sndimg.com/food/image/upload/w_555,h_416,c_fit,fl_progressive,q_95/v1/img/recipes/11/48/22/pic9WSjAU.jpg&url=https://www.food.com/recipe/asian-vinaigrette-salad-dressing-114822&description=This is simple to throw together, light and tangy. I often vary the ingredients, using honey or orange juice instead of sugar, adding wasabi powder -- whatever I'm in the mood for.

The joy of cooking is being creative!

If you wish to substitute the oil, use a bland vegetable oil, extra virgin olive oil or "light" olive oil. Regular olive oil is too strong for this recipe.> "Share via Pinterest") [![icons / email](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)](<mailto:?subject=Asian Vinaigrette Salad Dressing&body=%0AThis%20is%20simple%20to%20throw%20together%2C%20light%20and%20tangy.%20I%20often%20vary%20the%20ingredients%2C%20using%20honey%20or%20orange%20juice%20instead%20of%20sugar%2C%20adding%20wasabi%20powder%20--%20whatever%20I'm%20in%20the%20mood%20for.%0D%0A%0D%0AThe%20joy%20of%20cooking%20is%20being%20creative!%20%0D%0A%0D%0AIf%20you%20wish%20to%20substitute%20the%20oil%2C%20use%20a%20bland%20vegetable%20oil%2C%20extra%20virgin%20olive%20oil%20or%20%22light%22%20olive%20oil.%20Regular%20olive%20oil%20is%20too%20strong%20for%20this%20recipe.%0A%0AView%20it%20here%3A%20https%3A%2F%2Fwww.food.com%2Frecipe%2Fasian-vinaigrette-salad-dressing-114822%3Fnl%3Demail_share%0A> "Share via Email")

1. [![](https://img.sndimg.com/food/image/upload/f_auto,c_thumb,q_55,w_70,ar_1:1/v1/gk-static/gk/img/avatar/pie.png)](https://www.food.com/user/2000155284)

  
  
  ![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)
  
  
  
  This is a great starting point for a whole lot of creativity. Myself, I added 4 whole garlic cloves, a small Jalapeño, and a small shallot while leaving out the scallions. I substituted 1/2 tsp of dried ginger for the fresh ginger. I also added chili sesame and some capsicum to give this all some heat. The flavors were awesome over one of those pre-chopped bags of mixed cabbage, carrots, and broccoli plus a whole cucumber.... As I said, this is a great baseline recipe to riff on.
  
  ![icons / ellipsis / ellipsis-horizontal](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

  
  
  [jacob_hawley](https://www.food.com/user/2000155284)
  
  Reply

  ![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,) 3

  

2. [![](https://img.sndimg.com/food/image/upload/f_auto,c_thumb,q_55,w_70,ar_1:1/v1/gk-static/gk/img/avatar/pie.png)](https://www.food.com/user/350812)

  
  
  ![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)
  
  
  
  I didn't add the scallions or sesame seeds at the end, and I used 1/2t of dried ginger, but this was YUMMY! Served over a salad of field greens, chicken tenderloins, avocado, and mandarin oranges.
  
  ![icons / ellipsis / ellipsis-horizontal](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

  ![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)
  
  
  
  Delicious. I only cut sugar in half and used chopped chives instead of scallions, too many chives in the garden. I served over a shrimp, avocado, mixed green salad.
  
  ![icons / ellipsis / ellipsis-horizontal](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

4. [![](https://img.sndimg.com/food/image/upload/f_auto,c_thumb,q_55,w_70,ar_1:1/v1/gk-static/gk/img/avatar/pop.png)](https://www.food.com/user/42869173)

  
  
  ![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)
  
  
  
  I would suggest decreasing the amt. of sugar in the recipe as I found it to be pretty sweet. Otherwise I followed the recipe exactly and thought the dressing was delicious - very much like Trader Joe's asian vinaigrette but even tastier.
  
  ![icons / ellipsis / ellipsis-horizontal](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

  Reply

5. [![](https://img.sndimg.com/food/image/upload/f_auto,c_thumb,q_55,w_70,ar_1:1/v1/avatars/21_medthumb_hor.gif)](https://www.food.com/user/1056692)

  
  
  ![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)
  
  
  
  This is delicious... Having it for lunch on a salad with avocado, hearts of palm, artichoke, tuna, etc. salad. Whew... say that five times fast. ;-) I did use dried ginger, and about a teaspoon of that, as well as half grape seed oil and half sunflower oil, since I like the flavor. Buzzed this in the magic bullet about ten seconds. It does separate a bit, so be prepared to re-mix it, but no biggie. Thanks for sharing a great dressing.
  
  ![icons / ellipsis / ellipsis-horizontal](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

  
  
  [Queen Roachie](https://www.food.com/user/1056692)
  
  Reply

  ![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,) 1

### Tweaks

MOST POPULAR
MOST RECENT

1. [![](https://img.sndimg.com/food/image/upload/f_auto,c_thumb,q_55,w_70,ar_1:1/v1/fdc/img/placeholder/avatar.jpg)](https://www.food.com/user/2000363123)

  
  
  
  
  I used honey ~ about 1 tsp more. Also, safflower oil ~ about 1/4 cup. As I did not toast the sesame seeds, I added about 1/4 tsp toasted sesame oil. I used one whole scallion.
  
  ![icons / ellipsis / ellipsis-horizontal](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

2. [![](https://img.sndimg.com/food/image/upload/f_auto,c_thumb,q_55,w_70,ar_1:1/v1/gk-static/gk/img/avatar/sushi.png)](https://www.food.com/user/2002507186)

  
  
  
  
  added cilantro stems and leaves, used toasted sesame oil and splenda rather than sugar. on a romaine salad with cukes, peppers and cabbage and grilled chicken marinated in the dressing. I'll make this again I'm very sure!!!!!! No one left any for tomorrow!!!!!!
  
  ![icons / ellipsis / ellipsis-horizontal](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

  This is a great starting point for a whole lot of creativity. Myself, I added 4 whole garlic cloves, a small Jalapeño, and a small shallot while leaving out the scallions. I substituted 1/2 tsp of dried ginger for the fresh ginger. I also added chili sesame and some capsicum to give this all some heat. The flavors were awesome over one of those pre-chopped bags of mixed cabbage, carrots, and broccoli plus a whole cucumber.... As I said, this is a great baseline recipe to riff on.

4. [![](https://img.sndimg.com/food/image/upload/f_auto,c_thumb,q_55,w_70,ar_1:1/v1/pickle_media1/media/userphoto/110125/U21214285/Photo_Video_53823434473112639403134_medthumb_hor.JPG)](https://www.food.com/user/250238)

  
  
  
  
  Delicious. I only cut sugar in half and used chopped chives instead of scallions, too many chives in the garden. I served over a shrimp, avocado, mixed green salad.

  
  
  [AQueen](https://www.food.com/user/250238)
  
  Reply

  ![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

  

5. [![](https://img.sndimg.com/hgtv/image/upload/f_auto,c_thumb,q_55,w_70,ar_1:1/v1/avatars/59_medthumb_hor.gif)](https://www.food.com/user/347822)

  
  
  
  
  Very good recipe, its light, sweet and tangy. I used peanut oil and omitted the sesame seeds and scallions. Next time, like most fellow reviewers, I'll use honey instead of sugar and cut back on the oil (1/2 cup is a tad too much). Added to a mixed greens salad, with white onions, grape tomatoes and avocado (so yummy with the ginger/garlic twist) and served with Vietnamese Chicken Curry with Sweet Potatoes over basmati rice. Thank you for sharing this great recipe.

  
  
  [Cristina Barry](https://www.food.com/user/347822)
  
  Reply

  ![SVG Image](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)
